title: Machiavelli is the most misunderstood thinker of all time – Ada Palmer
summary: Dwarkesh Patel interviews historian Ada Palmer on Machiavelli's life and political thought. Palmer argues Machiavelli is widely misunderstood, explaining how his diplomatic experience in Renaissance Florence informed his insights on power and human nature, which he documented in his writings.
writeup: Dwarkesh Patel interviews historian Ada Palmer about Machiavelli's life and political thought, with Palmer arguing that he is widely misunderstood. The discussion traces how his diplomatic experience in Renaissance Florence shaped his observations on power and human nature, and how those observations made their way into his writings. The takeaway is a reading of Machiavelli grounded in his actual career and context rather than the popular caricature.
